clinical_coherence.notes: 70M with CAD, paroxysmal AFib, HTN, DM2, and CKD 3a presenting after witnessed sudden collapse while seated. No prodrome, no seizure activity, rapid recovery. HR 45 on presentation. Brother with pacemaker for sick sinus syndrome adds family history context. Vitals coherent with complete heart block (BP 116/63, HR 45). Small forehead laceration from fall is realistic.

tool_output_consistency.notes: ECG shows complete heart block with ventricular escape at 35 bpm -- perfectly explains the syncope. EEG normal. Labs show borderline troponin (demand), elevated BNP (265), glucose elevated (known DM). Holter confirms persistent CHB with 4.2-second pause. Cardiac MRI shows basal septal fibrosis at His bundle level (Lenegre-Lev disease). All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: Primary diagnosis and critical actions are excellent. ICD code R55 (syncope and collapse) is acceptable as the presenting symptom code, though I44.2 (complete AV block) would be more specific as the underlying etiology. Contraindicated actions (AEDs, beta-blockers, adenosine) are well-chosen.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is perfectly calibrated. Complete heart block on ECG with HR 35 is an immediately recognizable finding that directly explains the syncope. The clinical picture is textbook Stokes-Adams attack.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 70M with CAD, AFib, HTN, DM2, CKD, and prior PCI is a highly realistic cardiac patient profile. Medications (metoprolol, lisinopril, metformin, atorvastatin, aspirin, apixaban) are standard of care. Brother with pacemaker adds familial conduction disease.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case. Demonstrates cardiac syncope vs seizure differential, Stokes-Adams attack, complete heart block recognition, the importance of holding beta-blockers, and the need for permanent pacemaker. Cardiac MRI showing Lenegre-Lev disease adds pathophysiological depth.

issues_found: Minor: R55 as ICD code is the symptom code; I44.2 would be more specific for the underlying complete AV block etiology
strengths: Textbook Stokes-Adams attack with clear ECG diagnosis
Comprehensive follow-up outputs (echo, Holter, tilt table, exercise test, cardiac MRI, brain CT, brain MRI)
Cardiac MRI demonstrating Lenegre-Lev disease pathophysiology
